summary: Iter7 — Public Seller Profile endpoint + SellerProfile page rewrite + navbar-mobile-toggle testid all verified. 8/8 new backend tests PASS. 23/23 iter5+iter6 regression tests PASS. All 9 frontend acceptance criteria PASS on preview URL.

issues: PRD copy says 'Browse Marketplace' link but UI renders 'Explore Marketplace' — semantic equivalent, non-blocking. Update PRD or text for parity.

action_items: Optional: align 'Explore Marketplace' vs PRD-stated 'Browse Marketplace' wording on /seller/UNKNOWN page — pick one and lock the i18n key.

Optional polish: add granular data-testids on SellerProfile (seller-name, seller-verified-badge, seller-stat-rating/orders/products) — current page only exposes seller-profile-page, seller-products-grid, seller-product-{id}. Iter7 PRD did not require these, but they would improve future test stability.
critical_code_review_comments: Route ordering is correct in server.py: /api/sellers/trending (line 1224) is defined BEFORE /api/sellers/{seller_id} (line 1273). FastAPI matches in declaration order so trending is NOT swallowed — verified empirically by GET /api/sellers/trending?limit=2 returning {sellers,count} shape and NOT {seller,products}.
GET /api/sellers/{seller_id} correctly excludes Mongo _id via projection ({_id:0, ...}) on both users and products lookups — no ObjectId leakage in JSON.
Endpoint handles datetime serialization explicitly (joined + product.created_at converted via isoformat) — good defensive code that prevents 500s on JSON encoding.
is_women_owned is exposed publicly via the seller projection — good, matches the women-owned badge UI in SellerProfile.tsx.
Naming fallback chain (business_name → name → username → 'Muuzaji') is consistent between /sellers/trending and /sellers/{id} — ensures the trending tile name matches the profile-page H1.
products_count on the seller profile equals the active product count and matches what the grid renders (verified: 29 == 29 cards rendered for seed_seller_biz_salama).
orders_completed query filter uses status IN [delivered, completed, released] — correct semantic for 'completed'. Confirmed 0 for the seed seller (no orders in seed data).

Navbar.tsx hamburger now has data-testid='navbar-mobile-toggle', aria-label='Toggle menu', aria-expanded toggles false→true on click — fully accessible and testable. mobile-watches-link is correctly gated on isAuthenticated and reachable after login (verified manually with +255712345678/test1234).

i18n: All sp.* keys (sp.verified, sp.women_owned, sp.joined, sp.stat.orders/products/reviews, sp.no_rating, sp.no_products, sp.products_by, sp.products_count) translate correctly between SW and EN — verified empirically (Maagizo/Bidhaa/Imethibitishwa appear after SW toggle, Joined/Orders/Products/Verified after EN).
server.py is now ~4597 lines — still growing; refactor into routers remains backlog (not blocking).
test_credentials: Verified login with +255712345678 / test1234 — issues session_token, redirects to /dashboard, gates mobile-watches-link as expected.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data required. seed_seller_biz_salama already has 29 active products in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_iter7_seller_profile.py covers: (a) /api/sellers/{id} happy-path shape with real id from /sellers/trending, (b) seed_seller_biz_salama specific assertions (name contains 'salama', not 'mama biashara', products_count>=1), (c) UNKNOWN_ID → 404 'Seller not found', (d) route-ordering smoke (trending response shape ≠ {seller,products}), (e) regression on /api/products/public, /api/products/related/{id}, /api/auth/login, /api/watches list. Frontend Playwright verified: trending-sellers-strip → trending-seller-{id} → /seller/{id} → seller-profile-page → seller-products-grid → seller-product-{id} → /product/{id}. i18n SW/EN toggle on seller profile flips Maagizo/Bidhaa/Imethibitishwa ⇄ Orders/Products/Verified. Mobile hamburger (navbar-mobile-toggle) opens menu with aria-expanded toggle; mobile-watches-link only renders when authenticated.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found. The highest-risk change (route ordering for /api/sellers/{id} after /trending) is correctly implemented — FastAPI in-order matching plus the static '/trending' literal taking precedence over the parameterised '/{seller_id}' is empirically verified by both the iter7 test_route_ordering_trending_still_works and iter6 TestRegression::test_sellers_trending.
